  "decision": "fail",

  "percent_complete": 80,

  "confidence": 1.0,

  "order": [],
  "workable": [],
  "findings": [
    "Incorrect bolt grade installed (head markings do not match submitted A325 specification).",
    "Required pretensioning was not performed; joint was only left in a snug-tight condition.",
    "No physical evidence of pretensioning method (missing turn-of-nut match marks or DTI washer indications)."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Reject the current bolt installation and require complete removal of the non-compliant fasteners.",
    "Submit a non-conformance report (NCR) to the structural engineer of record and general contractor.",
    "Replace fasteners with the correct, pre-qualified A325 bolts and install them to the specified pretension using an approved method (e.g., turn-of-nut, calibrated wrench, or DTIs) under inspection."
  ],
  "rationale": "The design specifies pretensioned A325 bolts for a beam splice. The installed bolts are of an incorrect grade, and the crew failed to pretension them, leaving them only snug-tight. This is a major structural non-compliance that compromises the shear and slip resistance of the connection.",
  "references": [
    "RCSC Specification for Structural Joints Using High-Strength Bolts, Section 4.2 (Pretensioned Joints)",
    "RCSC Specification, Section 2 (Bolt Specifications)",
    "RCSC Specification, Section 8.2 (Pretensioning Methods and Verification)"
  ]
}
